title: Bulk report - Week 2
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market had a positive opening on Monday followed by consecutive declines on the timecharter routes. The Cape5TC fell over $2,000 this week, settling a tick above the $10,000 threshold on Friday. A few period fixtures were reported, including a 171,000-dwt 2013-built vessel open Caofeidian last week fixing for about six to eight months at 85% of the C5TC.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market had a positive opening on Monday followed by consecutive declines on the timecharter routes. The Cape5TC fell over $2,000 this week, settling a tick above the $10,000 threshold on Friday.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: It proved to be another week of further considerable losses for the Panamax market, retracting back to values witnessed in August 2022. With countless ballasting and spot tonnage unfixed, resistance from owners was scarce as tonnage far outweighed demand.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: The malaise continued across the board with limited fresh enquiry in most regions combined with an abundance of prompt tonnage keeping downward pressure on rates. Some brokers commented that with the Lunar New Year festivities coming at the end of next week this trend was set to continue.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: With limited enquiry across all regions, we have seen the erosion of rates continue. In East Cost South America, pressure remained from the larger sizes and a 38,000-dwt fixed from Rio De Janeiro to Japan at $15,000.
